rookie.title: Desert Vendetta
rookie.flavor: The Desert Oasis Hotel promised luxury and discretion for international clients. When arms dealer Viktor Kozlov was found dead in the presidential suite, someone had collected on an old debt.
rookie.emptyState: In the desert, some mirages are deadly.

items.name: Amira Hassan
items.detail: Hotel security chief with military background

items.name: Dimitri Petrov
items.detail: Russian businessman and Kozlov's former partner

items.name: Fatima Al-Rashid
items.detail: Wealthy socialite and arms trade victim

items.name: Poisoned Champagne
items.detail: Cyanide-laced Dom Pérignon from room service

items.name: Silenced Pistol
items.detail: Military-grade weapon fired through window

items.name: Nerve Agent
items.detail: Chemical weapon applied to door handle

items.name: Presidential Suite
items.detail: Luxury penthouse where Kozlov was found

items.name: Hotel Bar
items.detail: Ground floor lounge with surveillance cameras

items.name: Rooftop Pool
items.detail: Private deck overlooking the desert

clues.heading: Swiss Bank Records
clues.text: Fatima's accounts show a $2M transfer to a Chechen rebel group that Kozlov's weapons killed.
clues.alone: Fatima funded rebels killed by Kozlov's arms deals.

links.insight: Fatima had revenge motive and used her charity access to poison Kozlov's champagne.

clues.heading: Bellhop Statement
clues.text: Dimitri was screaming at Kozlov in the lobby about stolen money from their weapons partnership.
clues.alone: Dimitri publicly confronted Kozlov about financial disputes.

links.insight: Dimitri had motive but his alibi proves he couldn't have delivered the poisoned champagne.

clues.heading: Ballistics Report
clues.text: No bullet holes in the suite windows or walls, ruling out sniper attack.
clues.alone: No evidence of gunfire in the crime scene.

links.insight: The lack of bullet evidence and Amira's presence elsewhere eliminates the silenced pistol theory.

clues.heading: Toxicology Results
clues.text: Cyanide found in Kozlov's champagne glass and bloodstream, not on door handles.
clues.alone: Kozlov died from poisoned champagne, not contact poison.

links.insight: The champagne poisoning in the presidential suite confirms both weapon and location.

clues.heading: Charity Gala Access
clues.text: Fatima's foundation sponsored hotel events, giving her unrestricted kitchen and service access.
clues.alone: Fatima had special access to hotel operations.

links.insight: Fatima had revenge motive and used her charity access to poison Kozlov's champagne.

clues.heading: Security Footage
clues.text: Dimitri left the hotel at 8 PM, two hours before the poisoned champagne arrived.
clues.alone: Dimitri was gone when the fatal champagne was delivered.

links.insight: Dimitri had motive but his alibi proves he couldn't have delivered the poisoned champagne.

clues.heading: Security Logs
clues.text: Amira was handling a disturbance in the east wing during Kozlov's estimated time of death.
clues.alone: Amira was occupied elsewhere when Kozlov died.

links.insight: The lack of bullet evidence and Amira's presence elsewhere eliminates the silenced pistol theory.

clues.heading: Room Service Receipt
clues.text: Dom Pérignon delivered to presidential suite at 10:15 PM, fifteen minutes before Kozlov's death.
clues.alone: Champagne arrived shortly before Kozlov's death.

links.insight: The champagne poisoning in the presidential suite confirms both weapon and location.

rookie.epilogue: Fatima Al-Rashid's quest for justice became twisted revenge when she discovered Kozlov's weapons had massacred the Chechen village her charity supported. Using her foundation's catering connections, she poisoned the champagne meant to celebrate his latest arms deal. The wealthy socialite who dedicated her life to humanitarian causes couldn't bear knowing her donations had failed to save innocents from his greed. As Dubai police led her away, Fatima whispered that two million dollars in charity couldn't buy back the lives his presidential suite dealings had cost.

clues.heading: Swiss Bank Records
clues.text: Fatima's accounts show a $2M transfer to a Chechen rebel group that Kozlov's weapons killed.
clues.alone: Fatima funded rebels killed by Kozlov's arms deals.

links.insight: Fatima had revenge motive and used her charity credentials to access hotel operations and poison the champagne.

clues.heading: Hotel Manager Report
clues.text: Claims he saw Dimitri arguing with Kozlov near the service elevator around 10 PM.
clues.alone: Manager places Dimitri near service areas at crucial time.

links.insight: The manager's eyewitness account is contradicted by security footage showing Dimitri had already left the hotel.

clues.heading: Ballistics Report
clues.text: No bullet holes in the suite windows or walls, ruling out sniper attack.
clues.alone: No evidence of gunfire in the crime scene.

links.insight: Without climbing gear evidence and no bullet holes, external access methods are eliminated.

clues.heading: Key Card Logs
clues.text: Omar's master key was used to access the presidential suite at 9:45 PM.
clues.alone: Omar's security credentials accessed Kozlov's room.

links.insight: Omar used his master key access but had no motive, making him an unwitting accomplice rather than the killer.

clues.heading: Charity Foundation Pass
clues.text: Fatima's humanitarian credentials granted her unrestricted access to hotel kitchen and service areas.
clues.alone: Fatima had special access to hotel food preparation.

links.insight: Fatima had revenge motive and used her charity credentials to access hotel operations and poison the champagne.

clues.heading: Security Footage
clues.text: Dimitri left the hotel at 8 PM, two hours before the poisoned champagne arrived.
clues.alone: Dimitri was gone when the fatal champagne was delivered.

links.insight: The manager's eyewitness account is contradicted by security footage showing Dimitri had already left the hotel.

clues.heading: Balcony Inspection
clues.text: No climbing equipment or rope marks found on the presidential suite's exterior walls.
clues.alone: No evidence of external climbing access to the suite.

links.insight: Without climbing gear evidence and no bullet holes, external access methods are eliminated.

clues.heading: Gambling Debt Records
clues.text: Omar owed $50K to local bookmakers but had no personal connection to Kozlov.
clues.alone: Omar was desperate for money but had no motive against Kozlov.

links.insight: Omar used his master key access but had no motive, making him an unwitting accomplice rather than the killer.

detective.epilogue: Fatima Al-Rashid's quest for justice became twisted revenge when she discovered Kozlov's weapons had massacred the Chechen village her charity supported. Using her foundation's credentials to access the hotel kitchen, she poisoned the Dom Pérignon meant to celebrate his latest arms deal. Her charitable status provided perfect cover to move through the presidential suite undetected. The wealthy socialite who dedicated her life to humanitarian causes couldn't bear knowing her donations had failed to save innocents from his greed.

clues.heading: Swiss Bank Records
clues.text: Fatima's accounts show a $2M transfer to a Chechen rebel group that Kozlov's weapons killed.
clues.alone: Fatima funded rebels killed by Kozlov's arms deals.

links.insight: Fatima had revenge motive and used both her charity credentials and wine cellar access to poison Kozlov's champagne undetected.

clues.heading: Hotel Manager Report
clues.text: Claims he saw Yusuf carrying champagne bottles through the service elevator around 10 PM.
clues.alone: Manager places Yusuf moving champagne at crucial time.

links.insight: The manager mistook Fatima in kitchen attire for Yusuf, explaining his confused but sincere eyewitness account.

clues.heading: Ballistics Report
clues.text: No bullet holes in the suite windows or walls, ruling out sniper attack.
clues.alone: No evidence of gunfire in the crime scene.

links.insight: The absence of bullet evidence and blocked maintenance access eliminates external entry methods entirely.

clues.heading: Key Card Logs
clues.text: Omar's master key was used to access the presidential suite at 9:45 PM for 'routine maintenance'.
clues.alone: Omar's security credentials accessed Kozlov's room.

links.insight: Omar unknowingly enabled the murder by providing Fatima access, making him an unwitting accomplice to her revenge plot.

clues.heading: Wine Cellar Security
clues.text: Fatima's foundation donated rare vintages, giving her secondary access to bottle storage areas.
clues.alone: Fatima had special wine storage access through donations.

links.insight: Fatima had revenge motive and used both her charity credentials and wine cellar access to poison Kozlov's champagne undetected.

clues.heading: Kitchen Security Footage
clues.text: Shows someone in chef attire preparing champagne, but face obscured by steam and hat.
clues.alone: Unidentified person in chef clothing handled the champagne.

links.insight: The manager mistook Fatima in kitchen attire for Yusuf, explaining his confused but sincere eyewitness account.

clues.heading: Maintenance Tunnel Inspection
clues.text: Underground passages to presidential floor sealed for renovations since last month.
clues.alone: Service tunnels were inaccessible during the murder timeframe.

links.insight: The absence of bullet evidence and blocked maintenance access eliminates external entry methods entirely.

clues.heading: Text Messages
clues.text: Omar received urgent texts about 'helping the charity lady with special delivery' from unknown number.
clues.alone: Omar was manipulated into providing access for a delivery.

links.insight: Omar unknowingly enabled the murder by providing Fatima access, making him an unwitting accomplice to her revenge plot.

inspector.epilogue: Fatima Al-Rashid's quest for justice became twisted revenge when she discovered Kozlov's weapons had massacred the Chechen village her charity supported. Using her foundation's credentials and wine storage access, she poisoned Dom Pérignon in the cellar before manipulating Omar into delivering it to the presidential suite. Her charitable status and donated vintages provided perfect cover for the elaborate plot. The wealthy socialite who dedicated her life to humanitarian causes couldn't bear knowing her donations had failed to save innocents from his greed. As Dubai police led her away, Fatima whispered that some debts could only be paid in blood.
